title: Messaging Outcomes, Delivery Claims, and Evidence · Compiler Case: Command Event Document
summary: A team calls every background task reliable pub/sub, promises exactly-once delivery, and reports publish calls instead of stakeholder, ownership, loss, duplicate, latency, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the command event document increment in messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics.
Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Command Event Document · Theory Command Event Document
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 2893 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate local publish, broker ownership, and observable stakeholder effect; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Command Event Document contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Command Event Document · Theory Command Event Document.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Command Event Document · Theory Command Event Document, keep Command Event Document connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Build Command Event Document evidence · Theory Command Event Document
